body.title: Granny: prune dispatched_cycle.json on load — remove tickets no longer active
body.intention: I intend that dispatched_cycle.json never silently blocks re-dispatch of tickets that have returned to sprint status after a failed or interrupted sprint.
body.description:
dispatched_cycle.json accumulates ticket IDs forever. Tickets reset to sprint (e.g. after a failed sprint, a fake-done reopen, or a manual setstatus) remain permanently blocked because tid in dispatched skips them regardless of current DB status. Today we had to manually clear the file to unblock 26 tickets.

Fix: in _load_dispatched(), after loading the JSON, cross-check loaded IDs against the DB — keep only IDs still in status IN ('dispatched','acked','in_progress'). IDs in sprint/done/triage/hold/escalated/cancelled/closed are pruned from the returned set. This runs on every load (Granny restart + optionally each cycle via a periodic reload path).

**Affected files:** devices/granny/daemon.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; graceful degradation — if DB query fails, return the full loaded set (fail open, don't lose state)
**Scope boundary:** Modify _load_dispatched() only. _save_dispatched() and in-memory set behavior during a run are unchanged. Pruning is a load-time operation only.
**Completion criteria:** Given dispatched_cycle.json containing ['T-active', 'T-sprint-now'], where T-active has status=in_progress (DB) and T-sprint-now has status=sprint (DB), _load_dispatched() returns a set containing T-active but not T-sprint-now. Test: mock psycopg2.connect returns the status rows; assert returned set is {T-active}. Also test DB failure returns full set.
